title: About
text: Mono stereo-video imagery annotation provides useful information discovery, description management marine environment (Harvey et al. 2021), fish shark assemblages, also characterising benthic biota (Langlois et al. 2021; Williams et al. 2020). imagery can sourced variety platforms (e.g. stereo-BRUV, Baited Remote Underwater stereo-Video; stereo-DOV, Diver Operated stereo-Video). standardisation, archiving sharing annotation data synthesis can contribute understanding large spatial temporal scale patterns marine biodiversity inform management. GlobalArchive collaborative archive stereo-video annotations fish benthic assemblages, designed support data standardisation, discovery, sharing synthesis data. platform brings together sampling information image annotation outputs (Projects > Campaigns > Annotation sets) summaries count length data fish benthic assemblages (Syntheses) ready reporting. designed around core principles secure user access, standardised data import, capturing field sampling image analysis information. platform designed ensure FAIR data principles complied , also enable granular data access users can choose level open-access applied sampling, annotation Synthesis data. CARE data principles achieved enabling users control access data. stereo-video image annotation, data can directly ingested common software (e.g. SeaGIS EventMeasure) imported generic format, Quality Control checks (see CheckEM). Schema controlled Annotation data associated Campaigns samples organised within Projects. Curated summaries count length data fish benthic assemblages made annotation data GlobalArchive platforms, can published DOI versioned provide unchangeable source environmental reporting. Data can accessed via secure API R package, enabling efficient structured querying sampling, annotation, within Campaign, Synthesis data.

title: Glossary

previous_headings: Appendix 4 - Required format for upload and import
title: Import Formats
text: sample metadata file required Annotation Synthesis imports. Sample metadata must provided comma-separated values (.csv) file. recommend naming file using format YYYY-MM_Project-name_Method_metadata.csv (e.g. 2026-06_Abrolhos_stereo-BRUVs_metadata.csv) ensure compatibility CheckEM, although file names accepted. GlobalArchive requires specific columns (listed Table 1), including defined formats validation rules applied import (see example Table 1). unsupported columns ignored removed ingestion, original files uploaded available download. Coming soon… Table 1. Column formatting requirements sample metadata file, note tables described transposed (rows columns) formatting convenience. ✔ opcodes used define sample. DON’T include column required define sample. periods used define sample. DON’T include column required define sample. String YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD YYYY = four-digit year MM = two-digit month (01=January, etc.) DD = two-digit day month (01 31) T required literal character. hh = two digits hour (00 23) mm = two digits minute (00 59) ss = two digits second (00 59) TZD = time zone designator (Z +hh:mm -hh:mm) String (must Yes ) sample annotated count data included analysis? String (must Yes ) sample annotated length data included analysis? Table 2. First three lines example Annotation sample metadata file stereo-BRUVs Campaign used additional backwards facing cameras habitat annotation. opcode column used define sample.

title: GlobalArchive Manual and R Package
text: Mono- stereo-video imagery annotation provides useful information discovery, description management marine environment (Harvey et al. 2021). includes information fish shark assemblages, well benthic biota (Langlois et al. 2021; Williams et al. 2020). imagery can collected using variety platforms, including: stereo-BRUVs: baited remote underwater stereo-video stereo-DOVs: diver-operated stereo-video standardisation, archiving sharing annotation data syntheses can contribute understanding broad spatial temporal patterns marine biodiversity help inform environmental management. GlobalArchive collaborative archive video annotations fish benthic assemblages. designed support data standardisation, discovery, sharing synthesis. platform organises sampling information image annotations using following hierarchy: Projects → Campaigns → Annotation sets GlobalArchive also stores curated summaries count length data fish benthic assemblages Syntheses, ready analysis reporting. platform designed around core principles : secure user access standardised data import comprehensive field-sampling metadata comprehensive image-analysis metadata controlled data sharing GlobalArchive supports FAIR data principles allowing users control level access applied sampling, annotation synthesis data. CARE data principles supported enabling users retain control access data. video annotation data, outputs can imported directly commonly used software, SeaGIS EventMeasure, supplied using generic import format. Data first undergo quality-control checks using CheckEM. Schema-controlled annotation data associated campaigns containing samples, organised within projects. Curated summaries fish benthic count length data can created annotations held GlobalArchive platforms. Syntheses can versioned published DOI, providing stable citable source environmental analysis reporting. Data can accessed secure API GlobalArchive R package, enabling efficient structured access campaign, annotation synthesis data.

what: Retrieve all Data from a synthesis using the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_all_data
title: Retrieve all Data from a synthesis using the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_all_data
text: function retrieves metadata, count, length data CheckEM synthesis, processes data, saves results RDS files specified directory given name. also returns processed data environment allows users add zeros species present directory exist, created, user informed.

title: Retrieve all Data from a synthesis using the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_all_data
code: ga_api_all_data(   token,   synthesis_id,   dir,   include_zeros = FALSE,   file_prefix = NULL )

title: Retrieve all Data from a synthesis using the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_all_data
text: token character string representing GlobalArchive token API authentication. synthesis_id character string numeric value representing GlobalArchive synthesis ID data retrieved. dir character string specifying directory RDS files saved. include_zeros Either TRUE FALSE, like data include zeros species observed, warning: can create large datasets use lot RAM file_prefix Optional character string. supplied, used prefix output filenames (e.g. "prefix_metadata.RDS"). Default NULL.

title: Retrieve all Data from a synthesis using the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_all_data
text: list containing metadata, count, length data frames.

what: Retrieve Benthic Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_benthic_list
title: Retrieve Benthic Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_benthic_list
text: function retrieves list benthic species Global Archive API. makes API call specified endpoint using basic authentication, reads response Feather format, returns data data frame. resulting data frame cleaned renaming removing certain columns.

title: Retrieve Benthic Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_benthic_list
Retrieve Benthic Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_benthic_list
text: token character string representing GlobalArchive token API authentication.

title: Retrieve Benthic Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_benthic_list
text: data frame containing list benthic species. data frame includes columns species details, columns removed clarity.

title: Retrieve Benthic Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_benthic_list
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Retrieve the benthic species list using API credentials species_list <- ga_api_benthic_list("your_username", "your_password") print(species_list) } # }

what: Retrieve Count Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_count
title: Retrieve Count Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_count
text: function retrieves count data GlobalArchive synthesis using API call. allows include exclude life history information retrieved data processes data accordingly.

title: Retrieve Count Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_count
Retrieve Count Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_count
text: token character string representing GlobalArchive token API authentication. synthesis_id character string numeric value representing GlobalArchive synthesis ID count data retrieved. include_life_history logical value indicating whether life history information included retrieved data. Defaults TRUE. FALSE, basic species information included.

title: Retrieve Count Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_count
text: data frame containing count data retrieved GlobalArchive API. data frame includes count data various subjects, optional life history details depending include_life_history parameter.

title: Retrieve Count Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_count
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Retrieve count data including life history information count_data <- ga_api_count(username = "your_username", password = "your_password",                             synthesis_id = "your_synthesis_id", include_life_history = TRUE)  # Retrieve count data without life history information count_data <- ga_api_count(username = "your_username", password = "your_password",                             synthesis_id = "your_synthesis_id", include_life_history = FALSE) } # }

title: Retrieve Habitat Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_habitat
text: data frame containing habitat data retrieved GlobalArchive API. data frame includes species information merged benthic species list.

title: Retrieve Habitat Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_habitat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Retrieve habitat metadata from a synthesis habitat <- ga_api_habitat(username = "your_username", password = "your_password",                                     synthesis_id = "your_synthesis_id") print(habitat) } # }

what: Retrieve Length Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_length
title: Retrieve Length Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_length
text: function retrieves length data associated specific synthesis GlobalArchive making API call. data can optionally include life history information species. data returned processed format species information joined based synthesis data.

title: Retrieve Length Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_length
Retrieve Length Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_length
text: token character string representing GlobalArchive token API authentication. synthesis_id character string numeric value representing GlobalArchive synthesis ID length data retrieved. include_life_history logical value indicating whether include life history data (default TRUE). FALSE, basic species information returned.

title: Retrieve Length Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_length
text: data frame containing length data synthesis, optional life history information species data joined GlobalArchive. data frame includes following columns: australian_common_name: Common name species. family: family species. genus: genus species. species: species name. caab: CAAB (Codes Australian Aquatic Biota) code. length: recorded length. sample_url: URL sample GlobalArchive. other_columns: columns present Feather file removed.

title: Retrieve Length Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_length
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fetch length data including life history length <- ga_api_length("your_username", "your_password", synthesis_id = 1234)  # Fetch length data without life history length <- ga_api_length("your_username",                          "your_password",                           synthesis_id = 1234,                           include_life_history = FALSE) } # }

what: Retrieve Metadata from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_metadata
title: Retrieve Metadata from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_metadata
text: function retrieves metadata associated specific synthesis GlobalArchive making API call. metadata includes geographic coordinates, processed separate latitude longitude columns.

title: Retrieve Metadata from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_metadata
Retrieve Metadata from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_metadata
text: token character string representing GlobalArchive token API authentication. synthesis_id character string numeric value representing GlobalArchive synthesis ID metadata retrieved.

title: Retrieve Metadata from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_metadata
text: data frame containing metadata synthesis, including processed coordinates relevant information. API request fails, function returns NULL prints status code. data frame includes following columns: TODO - brooke add columns latitude_dd: latitude decimal degrees. longitude_dd: longitude decimal degrees. sample_url: URL sample GlobalArchive. other_columns: columns present Feather file removed.

title: Retrieve Metadata from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fetch metadata for a specific synthesis metadata <- ga_api_metadata("your_username", "your_password", synthesis_id = 1234)  # Display the first few rows of metadata head(metadata) } # }

what: Retrieve Relief Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_relief
title: Retrieve Relief Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_relief
text: function retrieves habitat data GlobalArchive synthesis using API call. processes data include relevant species information merging benthic species list.

title: Retrieve Relief Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_relief
Retrieve Relief Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_relief
text: token character string representing GlobalArchive token API authentication. synthesis_id character string numeric value representing GlobalArchive synthesis ID habitat data retrieved.

title: Retrieve Relief Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_relief
text: data frame containing habitat data retrieved GlobalArchive API. data frame includes species information merged benthic species list.

title: Retrieve Relief Data from the GlobalArchive API — ga_api_relief
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Retrieve relief metadata from a synthesis relief <- ga_api_relief(username = "your_username", password = "your_password",                                     synthesis_id = "your_synthesis_id") print(relief) } # }

title: Set GlobalArchive API Token — ga_api_set_token
text: function prompts user enter GlobalArchive API token, saves RDS file within "secrets" directory, optionally adds "secrets" directory .gitignore file function run inside Git repository.

title: Set GlobalArchive API Token — ga_api_set_token
text: function performs following steps: Prompts user input GlobalArchive API token via console. Creates "secrets" directory current working directory already exist. Saves token RDS file named api_token.rds inside "secrets" directory. Checks current directory part Git repository (.e., contains .git folder). Git repository, function adds "secrets/" directory .gitignore file already listed.

title: Set GlobalArchive API Token — ga_api_set_token
text: function intended used projects involving GlobalArchive, sensitive information like API tokens committed version control. Ensure working directory set correctly running function.

title: Set GlobalArchive API Token — ga_api_set_token
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Run the function to set a GlobalArchive API token   ga_api_set_token() } # }

what: Retrieve Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_species_list
title: Retrieve Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_species_list
text: function retrieves species list GlobalArchive making API call. data returned data frame relevant species information, excluding columns needed analysis.

title: Retrieve Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_species_list
Retrieve Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_species_list
text: token character string representing GlobalArchive token API authentication.

title: Retrieve Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_species_list
text: data frame containing species list GlobalArchive. data frame includes following columns: subject: URL identifier species GlobalArchive. australian_common_name: Common name species. family: family species. genus: genus species. species: species name. caab: CAAB (Codes Australian Aquatic Biota) code. other_columns: relevant columns included original dataset.

title: Retrieve Species List from the Global Archive API — ga_api_species_list
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fetch the species list from GlobalArchive species_list <- ga_api_species_list("your_username", "your_password")  # Display the first few rows of the species list head(species_list) } # }
